reasoning: This is the hardest call in the portfolio. The bear's point about bookings being a leading indicator is strong — management guided 11% bookings growth, which means revenue deceleration is coming. But the bull is right that at $95, the stock has already crashed 82% from ATH and the panic selling may have overdiscounted the bad news. The -65% loss is real, but at 1.6% portfolio weight, the position is small enough that selling here locks in a devastating loss for negligible redeployment benefit. The $400M buyback and $370M FCF provide a valuation floor around $80-90. I'll hold the 11 shares and wait for Q2 earnings (July 2026) to decide — if bookings re-accelerate to 15%+, the stock re-rates to $120+. If bookings stay below 12%, I reduce to 3-5 shares. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Add only if book
entryConditions: Add only if bookings growth re-accelerates to 20%+ AND stock reclaims 50d MA ($115)
exitConditions: Reduce to 3-5 shares if Q2 guidance confirms bookings stay below 12% growth
Full exit only if revenue growth drops below 10%.

catalysts.description: earnings (July 2026) to decide — if bookings re-accelerate to 15%+, the stock re-rates to $120+

bullCase: Duolingo is growing revenue 35% YoY at a forward P/E of 29x — a sub-1 PEG ratio that the market isn't appreciating. The bookings deceleration (24%→11%) is a deliberate strategic pivot to prioritize user growth over monetization, not structural breakdown. Director bought 5,000 shares at ~$99 on the open market. $370M FCF, $400M buyback (5%+ of market cap) provides a floor. All analyst targets still imply upside even after severe cuts. Trading at 0.8% of 52-week range = capitulation exhaustion.
bearCase: Bookings growth halved in one quarter — revenue is lagging, bookings is leading, and the guide always wins. At normalized 15-18% growth, PEG blows out to 1.5-2.0x and fair value is $65-85. Goldman slashed target 58%. Chart is catastrophically broken — 62% below 200d MA, recovery requires 160% rally. Director's $475K purchase is a token gesture for an $8B company. $400M buyback signals the growth story has faded — it's a mature company playbook. Already -65% P/L — dead money with opportunity cost.
